danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming bullets and energy blasts with contrasting colors to background
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate collision threat; player must dodge

danger_signals.visual_form: Solid barriers and maze structures in third-person stage 4
Large enemy entity with distinct visual presentation at stage end
Swarms of smaller enemy units with consistent design language
danger_signals.meaning: Sustained threat intensity; enemy wave pattern

danger_signals.visual_form: Minimized Vic Viper sprite after power-up loss
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state; decreased offensive and defensive capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Free-floating glowing objects scattered in stage 4 and throughout levels
Enemy-free corridors and gaps in bullet patterns
Visual indicator around player ship when shield active
Level completion landmark or final boss defeated

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy unit design consistent across swarms; boss entities have distinct visual differentiation from standard enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy spawn patterns and stage design reflect faction control of territories; stage-specific enemy compositions indicate faction military composition

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Vic Viper starfighter as player identity symbol
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player weapon customization visually differentiates load-out strategy; ship size and appearance remain consistent regardless of world state

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early campaign (Beginner mode vs Technical mode)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Identical visual presentation; difficulty difference is mechanical only
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Beginner mode: reduced enemy density and fire rate; Technical mode: escalated difficulty loop with increased enemy spawning and attack patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Camera perspective shifts from horizontal scrolling to behind-the-ship view; environmental walls become primary visual hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No enemies present; gameplay becomes navigation and resource collection; player must manage speed and positioning through tight corridors

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual callback to earlier game aesthetics; familiar enemy and stage designs from series history
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unlocked through skill-based progression; content reward for experienced players

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss entity visually distinct from standard enemies; typically larger sprite size and complex animation patterns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss telegraphs attack patterns through visual firing sequences; attack patterns escalate in complexity as battle progresses

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vic Viper sprite changes size or displays visual augmentation effects when enhanced
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player weapon output, defensive capability, and movement speed change based on collected power-up combination

environmental_storytelling.example: Different enemy types appear in different stages, communicating Bacterion Empire's varied military forces and territory control across different environments
Stage 6 in arcade version (cell stage) moved to final stage in SNES version with new layout and boss 'Beacon', signaling climactic confrontation
Intentional slowdown effect during intense enemy swarms and boss battles visually communicates danger and intensity escalation
Pseudo-3D environmental stages communicate alien/hostile spatial design; removed from SNES version but original arcade version uses this for world-building
Each stage boss has unique visual presentation reflecting different Bacterion military units and architectural design language

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Increased attack spread and fire rate; enhanced defensive capability; faster movement responsiveness
Reduced to minimal configuration; lower firepower; slower attack rate; increased vulnerability to subsequent hits
Standard formation patterns; predictable fire sequences; manageable threat level
Increased density; more complex attack patterns; overlapping fire trajectories; escalated threat intensity
Basic attack patterns; telegraphed movements; predictable vulnerable windows
Attack pattern acceleration; reduced vulnerability windows; final desperation phase with maximum firepower
Floats passively through stage; available for player collection; no threat

notes: Gradius III communicates world-state primarily through mechanical manifestation (difficulty scaling, enemy density, attack patterns) rather than explicit visual narrative. The Bacterion Empire exists as antagonistic force but lacks faction-specific visual markers like color schemes or heraldry. Player progression is communicated through ship configuration changes and stage advancement. The shift from horizontal scrolling to third-person perspective in stage 4 represents significant world-state change. SNES version differs from arcade in visual presentation (removed pseudo-3D stages, cell stage repositioned, new boss Beacon added) which subtly communicates different narrative emphasis. Slowdown mechanic serves dual purpose: technical limitation and intentional intensity communication.
